title: Are Iren's $5.5 Billion Deals With Nvidia Game Changers?
text: The Motley Fool analysis examines two major agreements between Nvidia and Iren: A Revenue Contract - A $3.4 billion, five-year cloud infrastructure deal where Iren provides managed GPU services using air-cooled Blackwell systems from its Texas facility. A Strategic Partnership - A warrant arrangement giving Nvidia the right to purchase 30 million Iren shares at $70 each over five years (currently out-of-the-money at mid-$50 prices). While the author acknowledges these deals validate Iren's neocloud model, he cautions that validation differs from operational execution. The stock's current valuation at approximately 56 times forward earnings already reflects positive expectations. The analyst recommends against chasing momentum at present levels, noting that whether the company can execute at the scale its valuation expansion increasingly demands is a question that won't be answered for some time.
